mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, increasing the present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, labor market deterioration signals weakening household income and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window, or mean return in trigger episodes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in both retail sales and industrial production indicate coordinated demand and supply strength that boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, month-over-month increases in consumer prices accelerate nominal revenue growth without immediate monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; this relationship inverts during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of low-rate inflation-acceleration episodes over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production relative to recent trend reflects improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO momentum remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months during walk-forward testing, or mean return in trigger episodes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in both nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm that employment gains are translating into consumption expenditure, amplifying the positive effect on corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-increase episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals abrupt monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window, or mean return in trigger episodes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
